step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the consensus that Option D (Codeine) is the correct answer. One additional point to support this decision is that many countries regulate codeine-containing products very stringently even beyond just requiring a prescription. For instance, they may impose limits on dosage amounts per container or restrict availability to pharmacies instead of general retail stores. These extra layers of regulation underscore how seriously authorities view the potential harms posed by codeine. Disagreeing here could overlook important aspects of public health policy surrounding controlled substances.
raw_response:
I agree with the consensus that Option D (Codeine) is the correct answer. One additional point to support this decision is that many countries regulate codeine-containing products very stringently even beyond just requiring a prescription. For instance, they may impose limits on dosage amounts per container or restrict availability to pharmacies instead of general retail stores. These extra layers of regulation underscore how seriously authorities view the potential harms posed by codeine. Disagreeing here could overlook important aspects of public health policy surrounding controlled substances.
